docs.title: Nifty 50 Tracker
docs.text: [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3.10+-blue?logo=python)](https://python.org) [![Streamlit](https://img.shields.io/badge/Streamlit-1.31+-red?logo=streamlit)](https://streamlit.io) [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT) [![Tests](https://img.shields.io/badge/tests-pytest-yellow)](testing.md)  **A real-time NSE & Nifty 50 market tracker built with Streamlit, powered by yfinance.**  [Live App](https://nifty50-stock-tracker.streamlit.app){ .md-button .md-button--primary } [View on GitHub](https://github.com/jyotheeswar012-max/nifty50-stock-tracker){ .md-button }

docs.title: What is this?
docs.text: The Nifty 50 Tracker is an interactive web application that provides a real-time window into the National Stock Exchange (NSE) of India. It tracks all 50 constituent stocks of the Nifty 50 index, NSE sectoral indices, and gives users tools to calculate portfolio P&L, explore historical snapshots, and visualize market trends.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Description Market Overview Live snapshot of NSE indices — Nifty 50, Bank Nifty, Nifty IT, and more Nifty 50 Index Candlestick / Line / Area chart with configurable time periods (1m–5y) All 50 Companies Live prices, 1-day change %, sector filter, and percentage-change bar chart Gainers & Losers Top N movers with visual comparison chart P&L Calculator Profit/loss + beta-adjusted market impact simulation Stock Chart Per-stock deep-dive with OHLCV chart for any Nifty 50 constituent Time Machine Travel to any historical NSE trading date (includes famous market events)

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text:
nifty50-stock-tracker/
├── app.py               # Streamlit entry point — UI orchestration only
├── pages/               # Additional Streamlit pages
├── utils/
│   ├── data.py          # Data fetching (yfinance, NSE)
│   ├── calculations.py  # P&L, beta impact, stock row building
│   ├── charts.py        # Plotly chart builders
│   ├── constants.py     # Nifty 50 tickers, index config, cache TTLs
│   ├── theme.py         # Custom CSS injection
│   └── supabase_auth.py # Optional authentication
├── tests/               # Pytest test suite
├── docs/                # This documentation site
└── mkdocs.yml           # MkDocs configuration

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/jyotheeswar012-max/nifty50-stock-tracker.git
cd nifty50-stock-tracker
pip install -r requirements.txt
streamlit run app.py

See the Installation Guide for full setup instructions.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: All notable changes to this project are documented here. Format follows Keep a Changelog. Versions follow Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Planned
docs.text:
• NSE holiday calendar integration
  • LSTM price prediction tab
  • Portfolio export (CSV/Excel)

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Comprehensive pytest test suite: unit, integration, and Streamlit AppTest smoke tests (tests/)
  • Full MkDocs documentation site with GitHub Pages deployment
  • CONTRIBUTING.md with development setup, branching strategy, and PR guide
  • pytest.ini with strict markers and short tracebacks
  • requirements-test.txt for isolated test dependency management

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Time Machine tab: travel to any historical NSE trading date
  • Famous dates preset (COVID crash, Budget 2024, etc.)
  • Beta-adjusted P&L impact simulation in the P&L Calculator
  • Sector filter on All 50 Companies tab
  • Auto-refresh every 5 seconds when market is live

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• safe_float() now handles pd.NA and None gracefully
  • Removed duplicate API calls in Gainers & Losers tab

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Initial release
  • Market Overview, Nifty 50 Index, All 50 Companies, Gainers & Losers, P&L Calculator, Stock Chart tabs
  • yfinance data pipeline with @st.cache_data caching
  • Plotly charts: line, candlestick, area, percentage bar, trend comparison
  • Streamlit Cloud deployment

docs.title: Contributing Guide
docs.text: Thank you for considering a contribution! This guide explains how to set up a development environment, run tests, and submit a pull request.

docs.title: Code of Conduct
docs.text: Be respectful, constructive, and inclusive. Disagreements about code are fine; personal attacks are not.

docs.title: 1. Fork and Clone
docs.text:
# Fork via GitHub UI, then:
git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/nifty50-stock-tracker.git
cd nifty50-stock-tracker

docs.title: 2. Create a Virtual Environment
docs.text:
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate  # Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate

docs.title: 3. Install All Dependencies
docs.text:
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install -r requirements-test.txt
pip install mkdocs-material  # only needed if editing docs

docs.title: 4. Verify Setup
docs.text:
pytest          # all tests should pass
streamlit run app.py  # app should open at localhost:8501

docs.title: Branching Strategy
docs.text:
main          ← stable, always deployable
  └── feature/your-feature-name   ← your work
  └── fix/bug-description
  └── docs/what-you-documented

Create your branch from main:

git checkout -b feature/add-nse-holiday-calendar

docs.title: Adding a New Feature
docs.text:
1. Write your code in the relevant utils/ module
  2. Add or update docstrings
  3. Write tests in tests/ (unit + integration as appropriate)
  4. Run pytest and ensure all tests pass
  5. Run streamlit run app.py and verify the UI manually

docs.title: Changing a Data Function
docs.text:
All data logic lives in utils/data.py. The function must:

  • Accept primitive types (no Streamlit state inside)
  • Return a clean pd.DataFrame or primitive
  • Handle failures gracefully (return empty DataFrame, not raise)
  • Be compatible with @st.cache_data

docs.title: Updating Documentation
docs.text:
Docs live in docs/. To preview:

mkdocs serve

Edit Markdown files under docs/, then preview at localhost:8000.

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
pytest                      # full suite
pytest tests/test_utils.py  # one file
pytest -k "TestCalcPnL"     # one class
pytest --cov=utils --cov-report=term-missing  # with coverage

All tests must pass before opening a PR.

docs.title: Submitting a Pull Request
docs.text:
1. Commit your changes with a clear message:

    git add .
git commit -m "feat: add NSE holiday calendar to market state detection"

  2. Push to your fork:

    git push origin feature/add-nse-holiday-calendar

  3. Open a PR on GitHub against the main branch.

  4. Fill in the PR template:

  5. What does this change?
  6. Why is it needed?

  7. How was it tested?

  8. A maintainer will review within a few days.

docs.title: Commit Message Format
docs.text:
We use Conventional Commits:

feat: add RSI indicator to stock chart
fix: handle empty DataFrame in fetch_intraday
docs: clarify beta formula in methodology
test: add edge case for zero-share portfolio
refactor: simplify build_stock_rows loop

docs.title: What to Contribute
docs.text:
Here are some areas where contributions are especially welcome:

  • [ ] NSE holiday calendar for accurate market-state detection
  • [ ] Export portfolio P&L to CSV/Excel
  • [ ] Dark mode toggle in the Streamlit app
  • [ ] Additional NSE indices (Nifty Midcap 100, Nifty FMCG, etc.)
  • [ ] LSTM price prediction feature (see Methodology)
  • [ ] Improve Time Machine performance with async fetching
  • [ ] Add more edge-case tests for build_stock_rows

docs.title: Methodology
docs.text: This page explains how the tracker fetches data, calculates metrics, and how the optional ML prediction pipeline is designed. Transparency here builds trust with users and contributors.

docs.title: Data Source
docs.text:
All market data is sourced from Yahoo Finance via the yfinance Python library, which wraps Yahoo's unofficial API.

User request
    └── Streamlit UI
            └── utils/data.py  ──── yfinance.Ticker.history(period, interval)
                                         └── Yahoo Finance API

Market Data Disclaimer

Yahoo Finance data may be delayed by up to 15 minutes. This app is for educational and informational purposes only — not financial advice.

docs.title: Market State Detection
docs.text:
The is_nse_open() function in utils/data.py determines whether the NSE is currently live:

  1. Converts current time to IST (Asia/Kolkata)
  2. Checks if today is a weekday (Monday–Friday)
  3. Checks if current time is between 09:15 AM – 03:30 PM IST
  4. Returns a tuple: (is_open: bool, status_message: str, last_close_label: str)

NSE Holidays

NSE market holidays are not currently hardcoded. The market-state check uses weekdays + trading hours only. Contributions to add a holiday calendar are welcome — see Contributing.

docs.title: Price Change Calculation
docs.text:
For all stocks and indices, the 1-day percentage change is calculated as:

% Change = ((Current Close - Previous Close) / Previous Close) × 100

This is implemented in utils/calculations.py → build_stock_rows().

docs.title: P&L Calculator
docs.text:
The calc_pl() function computes three values given buy price, sell price, and quantity:

Investment  = buy_price × quantity
P&L         = (sell_price − buy_price) × quantity
Return (%)  = (P&L / Investment) × 100

docs.title: Beta-Adjusted Impact
docs.text:
The beta-adjusted simulation (calc_beta_impact()) estimates how a given Nifty move affects a stock:

Stock Move (%) = Nifty Move (%) × Beta
New Price      = Buy Price × (1 + Stock Move / 100)
P&L Impact     = (New Price − Buy Price) × Quantity

Beta measures a stock's sensitivity to Nifty movements. A beta of 1.5 means the stock is expected to move 1.5× the index.

docs.title: Caching Strategy
docs.text: To avoid hitting Yahoo Finance on every Streamlit re-render, @st.cache_data(ttl=CACHE_TTL) is applied to all data-fetching functions. The default TTL is 60 seconds when the market is open, keeping data reasonably fresh without excessive API calls.

docs.title: Time Machine
docs.text: The Time Machine tab pre-fetches the full 5-year history for all 50 stocks in a single cached call (fetch_all_history()). It then filters to the closest available trading day to the user-selected date using build_time_machine_snapshot(). The first load may take 30–60 seconds; subsequent loads within the same session are instant.

docs.title: Future: ML Price Prediction (Roadmap)
docs.text:
A planned extension is an LSTM-based 5-day price forecast per stock. The intended architecture:

Component Detail Model LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) via TensorFlow/Keras Features Close price, MA20, MA50, RSI, MACD, Volume Window 60-day lookback to predict next 5 closing prices Training data 5 years of daily OHLCV per Nifty 50 stock Evaluation RMSE, MAE on hold-out 20% test split Uncertainty Monte Carlo Dropout for prediction intervals

Warning

ML price predictions are illustrative models, not investment advice. Past patterns do not guarantee future returns.

docs.title: Testing
docs.text: The project uses pytest with three test layers: unit, integration, and Streamlit smoke tests.

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
# Install test dependencies
pip install -r requirements-test.txt

# Run all tests
pytest

# Run with coverage report
pytest --cov=utils --cov-report=html
open htmlcov/index.html

# Run a specific layer
pytest tests/test_utils.py -v       # unit tests
pytest tests/test_pnl.py -v         # P&L tests
pytest tests/test_api.py -v         # integration (mocked API)
pytest tests/test_streamlit_app.py  # Streamlit smoke tests

docs.title: Test Suite Structure
docs.text: File Type Tests What it covers tests/conftest.py Fixtures — Shared OHLCV data, holdings, mock yfinance ticker tests/test_utils.py Unit 20 clean_ohlcv, returns, Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, MAs tests/test_pnl.py Unit 10 calc_pl, portfolio summary, edge cases (zero shares) tests/test_api.py Integration 13 Mocked yfinance: return types, column names, period passthrough tests/test_streamlit_app.py Smoke 10 AppTest: no exceptions, charts, KPIs, sidebar, error-free load

docs.title: Key Fixtures (conftest.py)
docs.text:
@pytest.fixture
def raw_price_data():
    """30-day synthetic OHLCV DataFrame."""

@pytest.fixture
def cleaned_price_data(raw_price_data):
    """Same data with DatetimeIndex applied."""

@pytest.fixture
def holdings_data():
    """4-stock portfolio: RELIANCE, HDFCBANK, INFY, TCS."""

@pytest.fixture
def mock_yfinance_ticker(cleaned_price_data):
    """Mocked yfinance.Ticker — no real API calls."""

docs.title: CI with GitHub Actions
docs.text:
Tests run automatically on every push and pull request via .github/workflows/ (see the repository). To trigger manually:

gh workflow run tests.yml

AppTest Requirement

The Streamlit smoke tests require streamlit >= 1.31. They are automatically skipped (pytest.importorskip) on older versions.

docs.title: Calculation Functions — utils/calculations.py
docs.text: All business logic — P&L math, stock row building, beta impact — lives here, independent of the UI.

docs.title: safe_float(value, default)
docs.text:
def safe_float(value, default: float = 0.0) -> float:

Safely coerces any value to float, returning default on failure. Prevents crashes when Yahoo Finance returns NaN or None.

docs.title: calc_pl(buy_price, sell_price, qty)
docs.text:
def calc_pl(
    buy_price: float,
    sell_price: float,
    qty: int
) -> tuple[float, float, float]:

Calculates P&L for a trade.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description buy_price float Price at which shares were bought (Rs.) sell_price float Price at which shares were sold / current market price (Rs.) qty int Number of shares

Returns: (pnl, investment, return_pct)

Return Formula pnl (sell_price − buy_price) × qty investment buy_price × qty return_pct (pnl / investment) × 100

Example: 

from utils.calculations import calc_pl

pnl, invested, ret = calc_pl(buy_price=2500, sell_price=2800, qty=10)
print(f"P&L: ₹{pnl:,.2f} | Return: {ret:.2f}%")
# P&L: ₹3,000.00 | Return: 12.00%

docs.title: calc_beta_impact(nifty_move_pct, buy_price, qty, beta)
docs.text:
def calc_beta_impact(
    nifty_move_pct: float,
    buy_price: float,
    qty: int,
    beta: float
) -> tuple[float, float, float, float, float, float]:

Simulates a stock's price change given a Nifty index move and a beta coefficient.

Formula: 

stock_move_pct  = nifty_move_pct × beta
price_change    = buy_price × (stock_move_pct / 100)
new_price       = buy_price + price_change
pnl_impact      = price_change × qty

Returns: (stock_move_pct, price_change, new_price, old_value, new_value, pnl_impact)

docs.title: build_stock_rows(data_5d, market_open, fetch_intraday_fn)
docs.text:
def build_stock_rows(
    data_5d: dict,
    market_open: bool,
    fetch_intraday_fn: Callable
) -> pd.DataFrame:

Builds the master table of all 50 Nifty stocks with current price, 1-day change, and sector. When the market is open, calls fetch_intraday_fn to get a live price; when closed, uses the last available closing price.

Returns: pd.DataFrame with columns: Symbol, Company, Sector, Price / Last Close, Change (pts), Change (%), _curr, _pct

docs.title: build_time_machine_snapshot(all_history, target_date)
docs.text:
def build_time_machine_snapshot(
    all_history: dict,
    target_date: date
) -> pd.DataFrame:

Filters the pre-fetched 5-year history to return a snapshot of all 50 stocks as close as possible to target_date. If the exact date has no trading data (e.g. a weekend), it falls back to the nearest previous trading day.

docs.title: Chart Functions — utils/charts.py
docs.text: All Plotly figure builders. Each function returns a plotly.graph_objects.Figure which is rendered in the UI via st.plotly_chart(fig, use_container_width=True).

docs.title: build_price_chart(df, name, period, chart_type, ...)
docs.text:
def build_price_chart(
    df: pd.DataFrame,
    name: str,
    period: str,
    chart_type: str,        # "Line" | "Candlestick" | "Area"
    y_title: str = "Price (Rs.)",
    height: int = 400
) -> go.Figure:

Builds the main OHLCV price chart for a stock or index. Supports three chart types:

chart_type Visual Best for "Line" Simple closing price line Trend at a glance "Candlestick" OHLC candles with green/red coloring Detailed price action "Area" Filled area under closing price Cumulative trend emphasis

docs.title: build_pct_bar(df, x_col, y_col, title, ...)
docs.text:
def build_pct_bar(
    df: pd.DataFrame,
    x_col: str,
    y_col: str,
    title: str,
    text_col: str = None,
    height: int = 350
) -> go.Figure:

Builds a horizontal bar chart of percentage changes. Bars are colored green for positive and red for negative values automatically.

docs.title: build_closing_bar(df, x_col, y_col, title)
docs.text:
def build_closing_bar(
    df: pd.DataFrame,
    x_col: str,
    y_col: str,
    title: str
) -> go.Figure:

Builds a vertical bar chart of absolute closing prices. Used by the Time Machine tab to display a snapshot of all 50 stocks on a historical date.

docs.title: build_trend_chart(series_dict, height)
docs.text:
def build_trend_chart(
    series_dict: dict,   # {"Index Name": {"df": DataFrame, "color": "#hex"}}
    height: int = 360
) -> go.Figure:

Builds a normalized multi-line trend comparison chart. All series are rebased to 100 at the start of the period so indices with different absolute values can be compared on the same scale.

Example input: 

series = {
    "Nifty 50":  {"df": nifty_df,  "color": "#0d9488"},
    "Bank Nifty": {"df": bank_df,  "color": "#6366f1"},
}
fig = build_trend_chart(series)

docs.title: Data Functions — utils/data.py
docs.text: All data-fetching logic is isolated in utils/data.py. Functions are designed to be pure (no Streamlit side-effects) so they can be tested and reused outside the UI.

docs.title: is_nse_open()
docs.text:
def is_nse_open() -> tuple[bool, str, str]:

Determines whether the NSE market is currently trading.

Returns: (is_open, status_message, last_close_label)

Return Type Description is_open bool True if currently within NSE trading hours (Mon–Fri, 09:15–15:30 IST) status_message str Human-readable status, e.g. "Market opens at 09:15 AM" last_close_label str Label for the last session, e.g. "Last close: Fri 13 Jun"

Example: 

from utils.data import is_nse_open

open_, status, label = is_nse_open()
if open_:
    print("NSE is live")
else:
    print(f"NSE closed — {status}")

docs.title: fetch_ticker(symbol, period)
docs.text:
def fetch_ticker(symbol: str, period: str = "3mo") -> pd.DataFrame:

Fetches OHLCV history for a single ticker.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description symbol str — Yahoo Finance ticker symbol, e.g. "^NSEI", "RELIANCE.NS" period str "3mo" yfinance period string: 1mo, 3mo, 6mo, 1y, 2y, 5y

Returns: pd.DataFrame with columns Open, High, Low, Close, Volume and a DatetimeIndex.

Example: 

from utils.data import fetch_ticker

nifty = fetch_ticker("^NSEI", "1y")
print(nifty.tail())

docs.title: fetch_intraday(symbol)
docs.text:
def fetch_intraday(symbol: str) -> pd.DataFrame:

Fetches today's 5-minute intraday candles for a stock. Used to compute live price when the market is open.

Returns: pd.DataFrame with 5-minute OHLCV data, or an empty DataFrame on failure.

docs.title: fetch_indices()
docs.text:
def fetch_indices() -> dict[str, pd.DataFrame]:

Fetches recent OHLCV history for all NSE indices defined in utils/constants.py → NSE_INDICES.

Returns: dict mapping each index symbol to its OHLCV DataFrame.

docs.title: fetch_all_stocks_5d()
docs.text:
def fetch_all_stocks_5d() -> dict[str, pd.DataFrame]:

Batch-fetches the last 5 trading days of data for all 50 Nifty stocks. Used to populate the All 50 Companies and Gainers & Losers tabs.

Tip

This function is wrapped in @st.cache_data(ttl=CACHE_TTL) in app.py to prevent redundant API calls.

docs.title: fetch_all_history()
docs.text:
def fetch_all_history() -> dict[str, pd.DataFrame]:

Fetches 5 years of daily history for all 50 Nifty stocks. Used exclusively by the Time Machine tab.

Performance

This call can take 30–60 seconds on the first load. Results are cached for the Streamlit session lifetime (ttl=3600).

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Create a .env file in the project root (never commit it):

# .env
SUPABASE_URL=https://your-project.supabase.co
SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=your-anon-key

docs.title: Streamlit Secrets
docs.text:
For Streamlit Cloud deployment, add secrets via the dashboard or .streamlit/secrets.toml:

# .streamlit/secrets.toml  (gitignored)
SUPABASE_URL = "https://your-project.supabase.co"
SUPABASE_ANON_KEY = "your-anon-key"

docs.title: Key Constants (utils/constants.py)
docs.text:
Constant Default Description REFRESH_MS 5000 Auto-refresh interval in milliseconds when market is live CACHE_TTL 60 Streamlit cache TTL in seconds for price data NIFTY50 list All 50 constituent stocks with symbol, sector, beta NSE_INDICES list Tracked NSE indices with display name and color FAMOUS_DATES dict Preset historical dates for the Time Machine tab

To add a new stock or change the refresh rate, edit utils/constants.py directly.

docs.title: Deployment on Streamlit Cloud
docs.text:
1. Push your code to GitHub
  2. Go to share.streamlit.io
  3. Connect your repo and set app.py as the entry point
  4. Add any secrets under Settings → Secrets
  5. Click Deploy

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.10 or higher
  • pip (comes with Python)
  • Git

docs.title: 1. Clone the Repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/jyotheeswar012-max/nifty50-stock-tracker.git
cd nifty50-stock-tracker

docs.title: 2. Create a Virtual Environment
docs.text:
macOS / LinuxWindows 
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate

python -m venv .venv
.venv\Scripts\activate

docs.title: 3. Install Dependencies
docs.text:
pip install -r requirements.txt

For development (tests + docs):

pip install -r requirements.txt -r requirements-test.txt
pip install mkdocs-material

docs.title: 4. Run the App
docs.text:
streamlit run app.py

The app opens at http://localhost:8501.

docs.title: 5. (Optional) Run the Docs Site Locally
docs.text:
mkdocs serve

Docs open at http://localhost:8000.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
streamlit_autorefresh not found

Auto-refresh is optional. The app works without it. Install with: 

pip install streamlit-autorefresh

yfinance rate limiting

If you see HTTP 429 errors, yfinance is being rate-limited by Yahoo Finance. Wait 1–2 minutes and try again. The app uses Streamlit's @st.cache_data to minimize API calls.
